title: The Daddy TGM – Retro 1970s Satirical Online Magazine

requirements.summary: Apply 1970s retro visual theme with warm earth-tone palette, retro fonts, aged paper texture background, and decorative elements
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette is strictly warm earth tones: browns, oranges, mustard yellows, avocado greens, burnt sienna — no cool blues or purples
Headings use a bold retro serif font reminiscent of 70s magazine mastheads
Body text uses a typewriter/monospace-style font
Background simulates aged newsprint via CSS (texture, grain, slight yellowing)
Decorative horizontal dividers and ornamental borders are used between sections

file_operations.description: Add 1970s retro theme CSS: import retro serif and monospace fonts (e.g., Cooper Black, Courier); define CSS custom properties for warm earth-tone color palette (browns, oranges, mustard yellows, avocado greens, burnt sienna); apply aged newsprint background using the generated image frontend/public/assets/generated/newsprint-texture.dim_512x512.png as a repeating tile with CSS grain filter, slight sepia overlay, and subtle vignette effect; add utility classes for decorative borders and retro dividers using the generated frontend/public/assets/generated/retro-divider.dim_600x40.png image

Extend Tailwind theme with 1970s color palette (brown, orange, mustard, avocado, burnt-sienna shades), retro font families (serif for headings, monospace for body), and custom background image utilities for newsprint texture and decorative dividers

requirements.summary: Display prominent magazine masthead 'THE DADDY "TGM"' with tagline on all pages
acceptanceCriteria: Masthead text 'THE DADDY "TGM"' is prominently displayed at the top
Tagline 'The Grooviest Rag on the Rack' appears below the masthead
Masthead uses oversized bold retro serif typography
Masthead is visible on all pages/routes

file_operations.description: Create Masthead component rendering the magazine logo using the generated image frontend/public/assets/generated/tgm-masthead.dim_800x200.png as a central image element, with 'THE DADDY "TGM"' text overlay in large retro serif font and 'The Grooviest Rag on the Rack' tagline below; style with warm retro colors and decorative border

Create Layout wrapper component that includes Masthead at the top and renders children content below; apply aged newsprint background and consistent padding/margins; this layout will wrap all page routes
Wrap all route components with the Layout component so Masthead appears on every page

requirements.summary: Implement top navigation bar with Home, Read Issue, Archive, The Soapbox, About links styled in retro theme
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation contains exactly: Home, Read Issue, Archive, The Soapbox, About
Active route is visually highlighted
Navigation is styled with retro 70s aesthetic
All navigation links route to their respective pages

file_operations.description: Create Navigation component with links to Home (/), Read Issue (/reader), Archive (/archive), The Soapbox (/soapbox), About (/about); style with retro 70s fonts, warm colors, and hover effects; highlight active route using React Router's NavLink; include responsive hamburger collapse for mobile
Include Navigation component below Masthead in Layout
Define routes for /, /reader, /archive, /soapbox, /about using React Router; ensure each route renders its respective page component

requirements.summary: Implement date-based daily issue system computing deterministic issue numbers from current date and tracking in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Today's issue number is computed deterministically from the current date
Same date always produces the same issue number
Each new date visited is saved to localStorage
Issue number is displayed on the Home and Reader pages

file_operations.description: Create utility functions: getIssueNumberFromDate(date: Date) computing days since a fixed epoch date (e.g., 1970-01-01) to return a deterministic issue number; getTodayIssueNumber() returning today's issue; saveIssueToArchive(date: Date, issueNumber: number) saving to localStorage; getArchivedIssues() retrieving all stored issues from localStorage sorted newest-first

requirements.summary: Build seeded pseudo-random content generation system producing consistent 50-page layout per issue seed
acceptanceCriteria: A seedable PRNG function is implemented in the frontend
Given the same date seed, the magazine always renders the same 50 pages
Different date seeds produce visibly different page orders and content selections
PRNG is used to shuffle/select from all content pools

file_operations.description: Implement a seedable PRNG (e.g., mulberry32 or xorshift) that takes a numeric seed and returns a stateful random number generator; export a function createSeededRandom(seed: number) returning { random(), shuffle(array), pickN(array, n) } for deterministic pseudo-random operations

Create generateIssuePages(seed: number) function using the seeded PRNG to select and order 50 content items from the content library; ensure page 50 is always a back-page item; return an array of 50 page objects each with { pageNumber, contentType, contentItem }

requirements.summary: Create large content library with 200+ satirical items across spoof movie posters, comic strips, fake ads, reviews, letters, back-page content
acceptanceCriteria: At least 200 distinct content items are defined across all categories combined
Each category has at least 20+ unique entries
Spoof movie posters include fake title, tagline, fake cast/credits, and ASCII or CSS art element
Comic strip panels have at least 2 dialogue-bearing 'panels' per strip, using CSS div layout
Fake product ads include product name, slogan, and humorous body copy
Spoof reviews include a movie title, star rating, and comedic review text
Letters to the editor include a fake name/location and a funny letter body
Back-page items are formatted as full-width/full-height page content

file_operations.description: Define a content library with 200+ items organized into arrays: spoofMoviePosters (30+ items with { title, tagline, cast, director, asciiArt }), comicStripPanels (30+ items with { title, panels: [{ dialogue, character }] }), fakeProductAds (40+ items with { productName, slogan, bodyCopy }), spoofReviews (40+ items with { movieTitle, rating, reviewText }), lettersToEditor (40+ items with { name, location, letterBody }), backPageItems (20+ items with { content }); all written in 1970s Mad Magazine satirical style with adult humor

requirements.summary: Build 50-page magazine reader with page navigation, page indicator, and CSS page-turn transitions
acceptanceCriteria: Reader displays 50 pages per issue
'Page X of 50' is shown at all times
Previous and Next buttons navigate between pages; Previous is disabled on page 1, Next on page 50
Page 50 always renders a back-page content item
A visible CSS transition (fade or slide) occurs when changing pages
Each page renders its content type with the appropriate layout template

file_operations.description: Create ReaderPage component: read issue seed from URL param or use today's issue; generate 50 pages using issueGenerator; manage current page state (1-50); render 'Page X of 50' indicator; render Previous/Next buttons (disabled appropriately); apply CSS fade or slide transition on page change; render current page content using PageRenderer component

Create PageRenderer component accepting { pageNumber, contentType, contentItem } and rendering the appropriate layout: spoofMoviePoster layout, comicStripPanel layout (CSS div-based panels), fakeProductAd layout, spoofReview layout, letterToEditor layout, backPage layout; style each with retro 70s theme and decorative elements using generated frontend/public/assets/generated/retro-divider.dim_600x40.png and frontend/public/assets/generated/starburst-badge.dim_200x200.png where appropriate

requirements.summary: Build Home page with today's issue cover, featured spoof poster, CTA button, and content teasers
acceptanceCriteria: Home page displays today's issue number and date
A cover-style spoof movie poster is shown as the featured cover
A 'Read Today's Issue' button navigates to the Reader at page 1
3–4 content teasers are shown below the cover
Each teaser links to the reader at the correct page number

file_operations.description: Create HomePage component: display today's issue number and date computed from issueUtils; use seeded PRNG to select a featured spoof movie poster as the cover image; render large cover section with masthead, issue info, and cover poster; include 'Read Today's Issue' button linking to /reader; generate 3-4 content teasers (truncated snippets from the issue's pages) with 'Read More' links to /reader?page=X; style with retro theme and decorative borders using generated images

requirements.summary: Build Archive page listing all previously visited issues from localStorage with links to read them
acceptanceCriteria: Archive page reads issue history from localStorage
Issues are listed newest-first with date and issue number
Each entry has a clickable 'Read Issue' link that loads the reader seeded for that date
Empty state message is shown when no past issues are recorded

file_operations.description: Create ArchivePage component: call getArchivedIssues() from issueUtils to retrieve all stored issues; render a list of issue entries showing date, issue number, and 'Read Issue' link to /reader?issue=NUMBER; sort newest-first; show empty state message 'Come back tomorrow for more groovy content, baby!' if no past issues exist; style with retro theme and decorative dividers using generated frontend/public/assets/generated/retro-divider.dim_600x40.png
Update ReaderPage to accept issue number from URL query param (?issue=N); if provided, use that issue seed instead of today's; ensure viewed issue is saved to archive via saveIssueToArchive()

requirements.summary: Build 'The Soapbox' community page with post feed, fake usernames, like button, reply threads, all persisted in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Users can type a message and submit a post
Posts are persisted to localStorage and survive page refresh
Each post shows a fake username, relative or absolute timestamp, and message text
Each post has a 'Groovy!' like button; clicking it increments the count and persists to localStorage
Each post has a 'Reply' button that reveals an inline reply input
Replies are displayed nested under their parent post
Feed is sorted newest-first
Fake usernames are generated deterministically (e.g., from a list of 70s-style names)
Page is styled in the retro 70s theme

file_operations.description: Create SoapboxPage component styled like a retro 70s social feed: load posts from localStorage on mount; render a post input form at top; on submit, generate a fake 70s-style username deterministically (from a predefined list), add timestamp, save to localStorage; render feed of posts newest-first; each post shows username, timestamp, message, 'Groovy!' like button with count, and 'Reply' button; clicking 'Groovy!' increments like count in localStorage; clicking 'Reply' reveals an inline reply input; replies are stored nested under parent post in localStorage and displayed indented below parent; style with retro fonts, warm colors, and decorative borders using generated frontend/public/assets/generated/starburst-badge.dim_200x200.png for like badges

Create utility functions for Soapbox: loadPosts(), savePost(post), likePost(postId), addReply(postId, reply); manage localStorage keys for posts, likes, replies; generateFakeUsername(index) returning a deterministic 70s-style name from a list

requirements.summary: Build About page with satirical fake founding story, staff bios, fake address, mission statement in 1970s voice
acceptanceCriteria: About page contains a fake founding story written in 1970s satirical voice
At least 4 fake staff bios with names and absurd credentials
A fake address and fake publication details are shown
A humorous mission statement is included
Layout mimics an old magazine editorial/colophon page

file_operations.description: Create AboutPage component styled as a retro magazine editorial page: render a fake founding story in 1970s satirical prose; list 4+ fake staff bios (editor-in-chief, staff writers, ad director) with absurd credentials; include a fake address and publication details; display a humorous mission statement; style with retro typography, decorative borders using generated frontend/public/assets/generated/retro-divider.dim_600x40.png, and aged newsprint background

requirements.summary: Ensure responsive layout optimized for desktop with mobile adaptations (vertical stacking, hamburger menu)
acceptanceCriteria: Layout renders correctly on desktop (1280px+), tablet (768px), and mobile (375px)
Magazine reader is readable on all screen sizes
Navigation collapses on small screens

file_operations.description: Add responsive behavior: on screens below 768px, collapse navigation into a hamburger menu using Tailwind responsive utilities or a custom toggle; ensure menu is accessible and styled in retro theme
Apply responsive padding and max-width constraints; ensure Masthead scales appropriately on smaller screens; stack content vertically on mobile
Ensure page content (posters, ads, comic panels) stack vertically and scale appropriately on mobile; adjust font sizes and spacing for readability on small screens
Add responsive typography and spacing utilities; ensure background texture tiles correctly on all screen sizes; add media queries for mobile-specific adjustments
